revision.text:
{{High-use}} {{Lua|Module:If preview}}
This module helps you find problematic usages of a template's parameters. Compared to [[Module:Check for unknown parameters]], you do not need to create long lists of parameters which should be categorised; it will automatically read them from the template's TemplateData. This means you don't need to define the parameter configuration in multiple places, which will inevitably lead to it being out of sync.
As well as that, it supports a few other types of errors, such as defining the same parameter multiple times, or using deprecated parameters (useful to do so you can replace usages before removing support for a parameter entirely).
== Basic usage ==
Add this to the bottom of a template: <syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext"> {{#invoke:Parameter validation|validateparams|module_options = Module:Parameter validation/default config}} </syntaxhighlight>
This will use the default config defined in [[Module:Parameter validation/default config]]. The module will begin to populate the following categories:
* Category:Pages using TEMPLATE_NAME with unknown parameters * Category:Pages using TEMPLATE_NAME with deprecated parameters * Category:Pages using TEMPLATE_NAME with duplicate parameters
For example, for {{t|Infobox station}}, it will populate: * [[:Category:Pages using infobox station with unknown parameters]] * [[:Category:Pages using infobox station with deprecated parameters]] * [[:Category:Pages using infobox station with duplicate parameters]]
Note that: * Unknown parameters means a page calling the template used a parameter that does not exist in the template documentation's TemplateData section * Deprecated parameters means a page calling the template used a parameter which, in the template's TemplateData, has the 'deprecated' option ticked * Duplicate parameters means a page calling the template and set a value for a parameter and one or more of its aliases
==Use in protected templates== Note that because templates' TemplateData code typically lives in unprotected /doc pages, protected templates that invoke this module can be made to incorrectly categorize pages by editors who do not have user rights sufficient to edit the template page itself.
== Full documentation == This module is based on idea and original code of [[User:IKhitron]].
The source of this module, along with the original documentation, can be found at [[:he:Module:ParamValidator]].
<includeonly>{{sandbox other|| <!-- Categories go here and interwikis go in Wikidata. --> [[Category:Modules that add a tracking category]] [[Category:Template metamodules]] }}</includeonly>

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: IMPORTING ALL WANTED MODULES
revision.text:
{{High-use}} {{Lua|Module:If preview}}
This module helps you find problematic usages of a template's parameters. Compared to [[Module:Check for unknown parameters]], you do not need to create long lists of parameters which should be categorised; it will automatically read them from the template's TemplateData. This means you don't need to define the parameter configuration in multiple places, which will inevitably lead to it being out of sync.
As well as that, it supports a few other types of errors, such as defining the same parameter multiple times, or using deprecated parameters (useful to do so you can replace usages before removing support for a parameter entirely).
== Basic usage ==
Add this to the bottom of a template: <syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext"> {{#invoke:Parameter validation|validateparams|module_options = Module:Parameter validation/default config}} </syntaxhighlight>
This will use the default config defined in [[Module:Parameter validation/default config]]. The module will begin to populate the following categories:
* Category:Pages using TEMPLATE_NAME with unknown parameters * Category:Pages using TEMPLATE_NAME with deprecated parameters * Category:Pages using TEMPLATE_NAME with duplicate parameters
For example, for {{t|Infobox station}}, it will populate: * [[:Category:Pages using infobox station with unknown parameters]] * [[:Category:Pages using infobox station with deprecated parameters]] * [[:Category:Pages using infobox station with duplicate parameters]]
Note that: * Unknown parameters means a page calling the template used a parameter that does not exist in the template documentation's TemplateData section * Deprecated parameters means a page calling the template used a parameter which, in the template's TemplateData, has the 'deprecated' option ticked * Duplicate parameters means a page calling the template and set a value for a parameter and one or more of its aliases
==Use in protected templates== Note that because templates' TemplateData code typically lives in unprotected /doc pages, protected templates that invoke this module can be made to incorrectly categorize pages by editors who do not have user rights sufficient to edit the template page itself.
== Full documentation == This module is based on idea and original code of [[User:IKhitron]].
The source of this module, along with the original documentation, can be found at [[:he:Module:ParamValidator]].
<includeonly>{{sandbox other|| <!-- Categories go here and interwikis go in Wikidata. --> [[Category:Modules that add a tracking category]] [[Category:Template metamodules]] }}</includeonly>

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "Category:Template" to "Category:"
revision.text:
{{High-use}} {{Lua|Module:If preview}}
This module helps you find problematic usages of a template's parameters. Compared to [[Module:Check for unknown parameters]], you do not need to create long lists of parameters which should be categorised; it will automatically read them from the template's TemplateData. This means you don't need to define the parameter configuration in multiple places, which will inevitably lead to it being out of sync.
As well as that, it supports a few other types of errors, such as defining the same parameter multiple times, or using deprecated parameters (useful to do so you can replace usages before removing support for a parameter entirely).
== Basic usage ==
Add this to the bottom of a template: <syntaxhighlight lang="wikitext"> {{#invoke:Parameter validation|validateparams|module_options = Module:Parameter validation/default config}} </syntaxhighlight>
This will use the default config defined in [[Module:Parameter validation/default config]]. The module will begin to populate the following categories:
* Category:Pages using TEMPLATE_NAME with unknown parameters * Category:Pages using TEMPLATE_NAME with deprecated parameters * Category:Pages using TEMPLATE_NAME with duplicate parameters
For example, for {{t|Infobox station}}, it will populate: * [[:Category:Pages using infobox station with unknown parameters]] * [[:Category:Pages using infobox station with deprecated parameters]] * [[:Category:Pages using infobox station with duplicate parameters]]
Note that: * Unknown parameters means a page calling the template used a parameter that does not exist in the template documentation's TemplateData section * Deprecated parameters means a page calling the template used a parameter which, in the template's TemplateData, has the 'deprecated' option ticked * Duplicate parameters means a page calling the template and set a value for a parameter and one or more of its aliases
==Use in protected templates== Note that because templates' TemplateData code typically lives in unprotected /doc pages, protected templates that invoke this module can be made to incorrectly categorize pages by editors who do not have user rights sufficient to edit the template page itself.
== Full documentation == This module is based on idea and original code of [[User:IKhitron]].
The source of this module, along with the original documentation, can be found at [[:he:Module:ParamValidator]].
<includeonly>{{sandbox other|| <!-- Categories go here and interwikis go in Wikidata. --> [[Category:Modules that add a tracking category]] [[Category: metamodules]] }}</includeonly>
